description:
You are a cloud-solutions architect who has just migrated the inventory service from an internal test subnet to its new production endpoint.  
Inside the workstation you will find the following file that was generated by the previous automation run:

/home/user/app/config/service.conf  
(current contents)  
------------------------------------------------
# Service configuration
service_name=inventory
endpoint=old.cloud.internal
port=8080
------------------------------------------------

Goal
1. Replace the value of the endpoint key with new.cloud.prod.  
2. Write a one-line verification log file named /home/user/app/config/migration.log whose sole line is exactly:
endpoint=new.cloud.prod

IMPORTANT EXECUTION RULES
• Accomplish both actions with a single terminal command (a compound command joined with “&&” or “;” counts as one).  
• Do not open an interactive editor.  
• Do not alter any other line or add blank lines to either file.  
• After running the command, you may display the two files to the console, but the automated grader will only inspect the file contents themselves.  
• The test suite will consider the task successful when:
  – /home/user/app/config/service.conf contains the line endpoint=new.cloud.prod (and no occurrence of old.cloud.internal).  
  – /home/user/app/config/migration.log exists and contains exactly the single line endpoint=new.cloud.prod with a trailing newline.

Use whatever non-interactive tool you prefer, but remember: everything must happen through one command invocation.
truth:
Initial state supplied to the container:

Directory: /home/user/app/config
Files and exact initial contents:

1) /home/user/app/config/service.conf  
# Service configuration
service_name=inventory
endpoint=old.cloud.internal
port=8080
(ends with a single trailing newline)

2) No file named /home/user/app/config/migration.log is present.

Verifier checks after the agent’s run:

A) /home/user/app/config/service.conf must read:
# Service configuration
service_name=inventory
endpoint=new.cloud.prod
port=8080
(with exactly one newline at the end of the file)

B) /home/user/app/config/migration.log must exist, be world-readable, and contain:
endpoint=new.cloud.prod
(with exactly one newline at the end of the file; file length = 24 bytes)

C) The exact string old.cloud.internal must NOT appear anywhere in /home/user/app/config/service.conf.

No root privileges are required; user “user” owns /home/user and everything beneath it.
